title: primula boothii subsp. repens
intro: Primula boothii subsp. repens, also known as creeping primrose or creeping wild rose, is a type of flowering plant that belongs to the Primula genus. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dyspepsia, due to its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Primula boothii subsp. repens include soothing digestive problems, reducing inflammation, and acting as a natural remedy for anxiety and stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
